highlight.title: Cloud Run Multi-Region Services GA & Enhanced Flexibility
highlight.summary: Google has made multi-region Cloud Run services generally available, allowing deployment from a single gcloud command. Combined with service-level minimum instances GA and new committed use discount coverage, Cloud Run now offers stronger cost predictability for production workloads.

resources.title: App Engine Max Instance Limits & TLS Deprecation for New Projects
resources.why_now: New App Engine projects after March 2025 default to 20 max instances (vs unlimited), requiring explicit scaling configuration. TLS 1.1 deprecation also forces security updates for existing apps.

resources.title: Secret Manager Cost Trap: Legacy VM Access Scopes Override IAM
resources.why_now: Legacy Compute Engine access scopes can block Secret Manager access even with proper IAM roles. The cloud-platform scope is now the modern standard for delegating full authentication to IAM.

resources.title: Pub/Sub Lite Discontinuation Creates Migration Pressure
resources.why_now: Pub/Sub Lite ends March 18, 2026, forcing migrations to standard Pub/Sub which can dramatically increase messaging costs without proper architecture planning and message lifecycle optimization.

resources.title: Cloud Run Functions Replaces Cloud Functions 2nd Gen
resources.why_now: Google renamed Cloud Functions 2nd gen to 'Cloud Run functions' in August 2024. Teams need to understand the unified platform benefits and pricing implications for serverless workloads.

claude_take: The biggest operational change is App Engine's new instance limits for new projects, which will catch teams off-guard during scaling events. Meanwhile, the Secret Manager access scope issue represents a classic cloud gotcha that can block deployments despite having 'correct' IAM permissions.
